qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Uncontrolled resource consumption via GraphQL blocks range query
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets the HTTP GraphQL blocks resolver loop at graphql/graphql.go:1319-1360, while the advisory patch targets p2p transaction handling of invalid blob/KZG-proof transactions in core/txpool validation and eth/fetcher/tx_fetcher.go.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. GHSA-mq3p-rrmp-79jg concerns high CPU usage from a malicious p2p message involving invalid blob/KZG proof transaction processing, fixed by surfacing ErrKZGVerificationError, stopping processing, and dropping the offending peer. The only final qualifying finding concerns an optional GraphQL endpoint blocks(from,to) range query with an unbounded resolver loop, which is a different entrypoint, component, and source-to-sink path.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-mq3p-rrmp-79jg describes high CPU usage leading to DoS via a specially crafted p2p message in github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum before 1.16.8.

Referenced commit abeb78c647e354ed922726a1d719ac7bc64a07e2 modifies core/txpool/validation.go and eth/fetcher/tx_fetcher.go to classify KZG proof verification failures as ErrKZGVerificationError, terminate transaction processing, and disconnect the violating peer.

graphql_blocks_range_dos/vulnerability_report.json describes POST /graphql reaching graphql.Schema.Exec and Resolver.Blocks, where graphql/graphql.go:1319-1360 iterates over a caller-controlled block range without a server-side range limit.
